name: Vía de la Plata and Camino del Sureste Loop
name_original: Benavente y Bretó desde Mózar – vuelta por los ríos Esla y Tera
description: This moderate 31.9-mile touring cycling loop combines sections of the Vía de la Plata and Camino del Sureste in Spain.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Villanázar are primarily characterized by the flat to gently rolling terrain of the Zamora province in Castile and León, Spain. The region features expansive agricultural plains and river valleys, notably along the Esla and Tera rivers. Historical greenways, such as sections of the Vía de la Plata, also provide dedicated paths for cyclists. This landscape offers generally accessible routes suitable for various touring cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Villanázar The most popular touring cycling route is Landscaping from Benavente to Morales del Río — Benavente y los Valles, a 30.2 miles (48.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 22 minutes to complete. This difficult route features notable elevation changes across the landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vía de la Plata Greenway Loop, an easy 10.2 miles (16.4 km) path. This route follows a historical greenway, offering a smooth and scenic ride through the area. Local touring cyclists also love the Rodeando el río Esla desde Benavente — Benavente, a 12.7 miles (20.5 km) trail leading through the Esla river valley, often completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes. Touring cycling around Villanázar is defined by river valleys, agricultural plains, and historical greenways. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Villanázar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 17 reviews. More than 126 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Villanázar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the overall difficulty level of touring cycling routes around Villanázar?
A: The touring cycling routes around Villanázar offer a mix of difficulties. Out of 29 available routes, 5 are classified as easy, 13 as moderate, and 11 as difficult, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes perfect for beginners or families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383208256/via-de-la-plata-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice, offering a smooth and scenic ride along a historical greenway. Another easy option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383246378/rodeando-el-rio-esla-desde-benavente-benavente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rodeando el río Esla desde Benavente — Benavente&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Esla river valley.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes near Villanázar?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 11 difficult routes available. One such route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383991697/landscaping-from-benavente-to-morales-del-rio-benavente-y-los-valles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Landscaping from Benavente to Morales del Río — Benavente y los Valles&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 48 km and features notable elevation changes across the landscape.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, you can find circular routes in the area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383208256/via-de-la-plata-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular easy option. For a moderate, longer circular ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036970854/alija-del-infantado-from-benavente-circular-via-arrabalde-via-verde-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alija del Infantado from Benavente – circular via Arrabalde – Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 67 km long.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Villanázar?
A: The terrain around Villanázar is primarily characterized by flat to gently rolling agricultural plains and river valleys, particularly along the Esla and Tera rivers. You'll also find historical greenways, such as sections of the Vía de la Plata, which offer dedicated, smoother paths.

Q: What points of interest can I see along touring cycling routes near Villanázar?
A: While cycling, you can encounter several interesting structures, particularly bridges. These include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge of the Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2810181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Esla River&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422594" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de Azoague Iron Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2720898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benavente&lt;/a&gt; and its &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María del Azogue&lt;/a&gt; are also notable landmarks in the vicinity.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Villanázar?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from 17 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes river valleys, agricultural plains, and historical greenways, offering options for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036970854/alija-del-infantado-from-benavente-circular-via-arrabalde-via-verde-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alija del Infantado from Benavente – circular via Arrabalde – Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; span over 67 km. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037068130/tera-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tera Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 56 km.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling around Villanázar?
A: Given the region's agricultural plains and river valleys, spring and autumn are generally ideal for touring cycling around Villanázar. The weather is typically milder, avoiding the potential heat of summer and the colder, wetter conditions of winter, making for a more comfortable ride.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in total around Villanázar?
A: There are 29 touring cycling routes available around Villanázar, offering a wide range of options for different preferences and skill levels.

Q: Are there routes that follow river valleys?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the area utilize the river valleys. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383246378/rodeando-el-rio-esla-desde-benavente-benavente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rodeando el río Esla desde Benavente — Benavente&lt;/a&gt; trail leads through the Esla river valley, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037068130/tera-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tera Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt; explores the Tera river valley.
tips: Mózar is a small town located at the confluence of the Tera and Órbigo rivers in the Benavente valley. Famous for its natural activities, there are several campsites near the river beaches for bathing and abundant places for trout fishing.

Castle-fortress from the 12th century located on top of a hill overlooking the surrounding landscape. Since 1972 it has been owned by the Parador Nacional de Turismo group and functions as a vacation hotel.
In the historic center, the Church of Santa María del Azogue, from the 16th century, and in Gothic style, stands out as the main monument of Benavente.

This bridge is one of the most popular icons of the Esla greenway. The combination of the wooden floor and the old metallic railway structure simulate a perfect tunnel and are very attractive for walkers and cyclists.
A beautiful picture when we cross this bridge on the Vía Verde del Oeste or better known as the Vía Verde de la Plata. A luxury for all lovers of hiking, gravel or mountain biking.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle loop typically takes around 3.5 hours to complete. This duration is for the specific 51-kilometer route and can vary based on your cycling pace and how often you stop to enjoy the sights.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Vía de la Plata and Camino del Sureste Loop is rated as moderate. It features approximately 218 meters of elevation gain and loss, making it suitable for cyclists with a good level of fitness, but not overly strenuous.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best for this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the varied surfaces often found on sections of these routes, a hybrid bike with 38mm tires or a mountain bike is generally recommended. Road bikes with narrow tires are not ideal. An e-MTB could also be a good option for added assistance.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This loop offers a blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You can visit the Mózar River Beach on the Tera River, explore the historic Benavente Castle – Tower of the Snail, and admire the Church of Santa María del Azogue. The route also crosses the Bridge over the Esla River and the Bridge of the Vía Verde de la Plata.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant times to cycle in this region, offering milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, which might make longer rides more challenging. Always check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Is this cycling loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While rated moderate, the 51-kilometer distance and some elevation changes mean it's best for cyclists with a reasonable level of fitness. Beginners who are comfortable with similar distances and some hills could enjoy it, but it's not an entry-level flat ride.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The Komoot tour starts and ends in Benavente. Specific parking information isn't provided, but Benavente is a town with various parking options. It's advisable to check local parking facilities upon arrival.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on many public trails in Spain, especially in rural areas, but leash rules and specific restrictions can vary. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved, on a leash where required, and that you clean up after them. Be mindful of other cyclists and potential livestock.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this loop?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this public route. However, if you plan to visit any attractions along the way, such as Benavente Castle, they may have their own entry fees.

faqs.question: Does this loop intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of larger, well-known paths. You will find it passes through parts of the Vía de la Plata hiking - 03 Salamanca to Astorga, Mtb Vía de la Plata, and the Camino de Santiago del Sureste, among others.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on sections of the Vía de la Plata and Camino del Sureste can be varied, including asphalt, gravel, concrete, and dirt paths. This diversity is why a hybrid or mountain bike is recommended for comfort and capability.
